title: Red Hat Security Advisory: Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform 7.1.13 on RHEL 7 security update
overview:
A security update is now available for Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform 7.1 for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform 7.1 EUS for RHEL 7 Server
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform 7.1.13 on RHEL 7 security update

A security update is now available for Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform 7.1 for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

A security update is now available for Red Hat JBoss Enterprise Application Platform 7.1 for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
eap7-jackson-annotations-2.8.11-2.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm
eap7-jackson-core-2.8.11-3.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm

eap7-jackson-module-jaxb-annotations-2.8.11-3.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm
eap7-jackson-modules-java8-2.8.11-2.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm
eap7-netty-4.1.63-3.Final_redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm
eap7-undertow-1.4.18-18.SP16_redhat_00001.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm
eap7-wildfly-7.1.13-6.GA_redhat_00002.1.ep7.el7.src.rpm
eap7-jackson-annotations-2.8.11-2.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-core-2.8.11-3.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-datatype-jdk8-2.8.11-2.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-datatype-jsr310-2.8.11-2.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-jaxrs-base-2.8.11-3.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-jaxrs-json-provider-2.8.11-3.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-module-jaxb-annotations-2.8.11-3.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-jackson-modules-java8-2.8.11-2.redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-netty-4.1.63-3.Final_redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-netty-all-4.1.63-3.Final_redhat_00004.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-undertow-1.4.18-18.SP16_redhat_00001.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-wildfly-7.1.13-6.GA_redhat_00002.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm
eap7-wildfly-modules-7.1.13-6.GA_redhat_00002.1.ep7.el7.noarch.rpm

CVE-2025-52999: A nested data handling flaw was found in Jackson Core. When parsing particularly deeply nested data structures, a StackoverflowError can occur.

CVE-2025-55163: A flaw was found in Netty where malformed client requests can trigger server-side stream resets without triggering abuse counters. This issue, referred to as the "MadeYouReset" attack, allows malicious clients to induce excessive server workload by repeatedly causing server-side stream aborts. While not a protocol bug, this highlights a common implementation weakness that can be exploited to cause a denial of service (DoS).
